steps.title: Use the side ratio
steps.content:
In an isosceles right triangle, if each leg is $a$, the hypotenuse is $a\sqrt{2}$. The perimeter is
$$
a+a+a\sqrt{2}=a(2+\sqrt{2})
$$

steps.title: Solve for the leg
steps.content:
Set the perimeter equal to the given value.
$$
a(2+\sqrt{2})=20+20\sqrt{2}
$$
$$
a=10\sqrt{2}
$$

steps.title: Find the hypotenuse
steps.content: Multiply the leg by $\sqrt{2}$. $$ (10\sqrt{2})(\sqrt{2})=20 $$ The answer is C.
